title: Microsoft Build opening keynote
sortTitle: microsoft build opening keynote
description:
Satya Nadella and Microsoft leaders share how Microsoft is creating new opportunity for developers across our platforms in this era of AI.

There is limited seating in the Festival Pavillion for the keynote. Please arrive early to secure a spot. Additional keynote viewing areas will be available in the outdoor theater, BATS Theater (building B), Cowell Theater, and inside Gateway Pavillion, level 2, at all theaters.
aiDescription: Microsoft Build’s core message is AI as a platform shift: The keynote frames AI as a major shift for developers, focusing on how they can build value in the emerging “frontier intelligence ecosystem.”  Microsoft presents a full AI stack: The stack includes compute fabric, models, tools, runtimes for agents/apps, developer tooling, and security, compliance, and governance.  Windows is positioned as an edge AI platform: Microsoft emphasizes the vast compute available across PCs through CPUs, GPUs, and NPUs, aiming to bring AI directly to devices.  The company’s vision is “unmetered intelligence”: Microsoft wants AI to run broadly across homes and workplaces using local and cloud compute, making intelligence always available.  Local AI on Windows is expanding: Microsoft highlights onboard AI already used in apps like Outlook, PowerPoint, Teams, and Adobe products, and announces broader Windows ML/AI support.  New AI hardware and models were announced: Microsoft introduced new Windows devices like the Surface RTX Spark Dev Box, plus local AI models for reasoning and planning to support agentic workflows.  Azure infrastructure is being redesigned for AI workloads: The keynote details major data center expansion, AI-optimized architecture, new silicon like Maya and Cobalt, and close collaboration with NVIDIA.  A new enterprise intelligence layer called Microsoft IQ is introduced: This combines Web IQ, Fabric IQ, and Work IQ to ground agents in web data, enterprise data, and organizational knowledge.  Microsoft is building a full agent platform: With Foundry, GitHub Copilot, Agent 365, execution containers, and autopilot-style agents, Microsoft aims to support secure, scalable, enterprise-ready agents.  The keynote looks beyond apps to scientific discovery and quantum computing: Microsoft showcases Microsoft Discovery for AI-driven science workflows and announces Majorana 2, highlighting progress toward scalable quantum computing.

title: Backing the future of innovation
sortTitle: backing the future of innovation
description:
Join Charles Lamanna with the founders of Mantis VC—also known as The Chainsmokers—for a candid conversation on what’s shaping the next wave of innovation.
 
From building and backing companies to spotting early signals in AI, the discussion will explore what’s gaining real traction, what’s overhyped, and where builders should focus next. Expect honest perspectives on how technology, culture, and go-to-market distribution come together to turn ideas into lasting products.

speakerNames: Patrick Brown, Rohan Ganesh, Surya Kukkapalli, Troy McBride, Vivaan Sawant, Advika Vuppala, Hans Yang
title: Imagine Cup World Championship
sortTitle: imagine cup world championship
description: The final moment is here. Three startups. One stage. This year’s finalists take the stage pitching their solutions as they compete to become the 2026 Imagine Cup World Champion, win $100,000 USD, and a mentorship session with Microsoft Chairman and CEO, Satya Nadella. This is where ideas meet execution and where student founders show what it looks like to build with scale in mind.
aiDescription:
Opening and Founders' Reflections: The video begins with brief introductions from previous Imagine Cup participants sharing their work and experiences. One founder explains that their company, Caduce, is focused on improving insurance reimbursement documentation for healthcare providers (00:00:33), while another describes Prada Health, a telehealth addiction clinic leveraging AI to improve quality of care (00:00:38). They credit mentorship sessions, especially one with Satya Nadella, for helping them refine their business approach. The reflections emphasize how the competition accelerated their understanding of scaling systems and deploying their ideas. One founder expresses hope that new teams will gain just as much from participating in the Imagine Cup and Microsoft Build experiences (00:01:04).

Introduction to Imagine Cup Finals: Host Donna Sarkar takes the stage to welcome the audience to Microsoft Build (00:01:31). She celebrates Imagine Cup’s 24-year history of supporting global student founders by connecting them with mentorship, startup ecosystems, and advanced technology resources (00:01:45). Donna highlights this year’s collaboration with Microsoft for Startups, GitHub Education, and Replit, which helped participants refine and ship their solutions intentionally. From over a thousand submissions, three startups—CopyFlag, Spoilsafe, and Revora Health—rose to the top to compete in the World Championship round (00:03:06). Donna introduces the prestigious judging panel and announces that the winning team will receive $100,000 and a mentorship session with Satya Nadella (00:03:01).

Finalist Introductions and Projects Overview: The video transitions to short documentary-style vignettes introducing each finalist and their projects. Patrick, founder of CopyFlag, recounts losing significant income due to copyright infringement, which inspired him to create an AI-powered protection platform for independent creators (00:03:16). Spoilsafe’s team discusses global food waste inefficiencies and presents their sensor-based AI system designed to predict spoilage across supply chains, improving distribution and sustainability outcomes (00:04:17). The founders of Revora Health explain their platform for connecting patients with physical therapists quickly and affordably, leveraging AI to personalize care and reduce provider fatigue (00:05:21).

Live Demonstrations: During the live portion of the event, each team presents their demo to the audience and judges. CopyFlag showcases how users can upload content for fingerprinting and AI-driven search to detect and remove stolen works (00:06:45). It features real-time alerts, predictive models like “Horizon,” and pattern mapping for repeat offenders, turning reactive copyright responses into predictive defense mechanisms. Spoilsafe’s founders demonstrate how their sensors gauge environmental conditions like temperature and humidity to predict spoilage for specific foods, providing actionable decisions that help warehouse teams prevent waste (00:08:21). Revora Health’s team illustrates an intuitive interface where users interact with an AI assistant named Clara to assess injuries and receive dynamically updated therapy plans shared with physical therapists in real time (00:09:43).

Founder Insights and Mentorship Session: Hans Yang, Vice President at Microsoft for Startups, hosts a discussion to uncover lessons from the finalists (00:11:23). Surya from Revora shares that trust in healthcare is built from the ground up and emphasizes co-designing with therapists to create validated, practical solutions (00:12:11). Rohan from Spoilsafe reflects on a pivotal customer insight—“We don’t need more data, we need actions”—that shifted their focus from selling visibility to selling confidence (00:13:07). Patrick from CopyFlag discusses transforming personal loss into an innovative venture that helps creators visualize and combat unseen forms of digital theft (00:14:03). Hans praises their clarity, drive, and problem-solving acumen, underscoring their symbolic role as model founders.

Announcement and Conclusion: As the excitement peaks, Donna and Hans congratulate the teams and acknowledge their mentors for helping shape their journeys (00:15:03). Hans reveals a surprise announcement: Microsoft has increased the grand prize from $100,000 to $150,000 (00:16:07). Finally, amid cheers from the audience, CopyFlag is declared the 2026 Imagine Cup World Champion (00:16:19). The program concludes by celebrating innovation, resilience, and the spirit of global entrepreneurship that Imagine Cup continues to foster.
ogTitle: Imagine Cup World Championship
ogDescription: The final moment is here. Three startups. One stage. This year’s finalists take the stage pitching their solutions as they compete to become the 2026 Imagine Cup World Champion, win $100,000 USD, and a mentorship session with Microsoft Chairman and CEO, Satya Nadella. This is where ideas meet execution and where student founders show what it looks like to build with scale in mind.

title: Agent supervision is the new senior engineering skill
sortTitle: agent supervision is the new senior engineering skill
description:
As AI agents start shipping code, the bottleneck becomes trust. This talk argues “agent supervision” is the new senior skill: scoping agent work, setting constraints, designing checkpoints, and reviewing outputs for correctness and security. Learn practical patterns to catch silent failures and build workflows where humans stay accountable and agents stay fast.

sortTitle: software defensibility in the era of ai coding

speakerNames: Alberto Martinez, Jaclynne Kearney, Rajan Mistry, Morris Novello, Alison Swift
title: Stop routing docstrings to 70B models with on-device AI on Snapdragon
sortTitle: stop routing docstrings to 70b models with on-device ai on snapdragon
description:
Your AI coding assistant calls a 70B+ cloud model just to add a docstring. Snapdragon X2 Elite’s 80 TOPS NPU changes that. In this session, build a three-tier inference routing architecture—on‑device (≤13B), on‑prem (14B–34B), and cloud (70B+)—cutting cloud tokens by 67%, latency by 70%, and keeping most code local. Includes routing logic, quantization trade‑offs, and a deployable classifier.

AI isn't just autocompleting your code anymore — it's writing plans, shipping PRs, fixing pipelines, and patching prod. In this demo-heavy session, we'll show AI agents working across the entire dev lifecycle: planning, coding, CI/CD, and live operations. You'll see how to move faster, keep agents on a leash, and build systems that fix themselves.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

speakerNames: Patrick Nikoletich, Steve Sanderson, Julia Gvero, Rachel Kloster
title: Your agent, anywhere: MultiClient, MultiDevice with GitHub Copilot SDK
sortTitle: your agent, anywhere: multiclient, multidevice with github copilot sdk
description:
Agents are powerful on your machine, but what happens when you need them everywhere else? In this session, we'll show how GitHub Copilot SDK lets you build an agent, embed it in an app, and take it with you across devices and into the cloud. You'll see how to go from a local agent to one you can access on your phone, move between machines, and run across multiple clients. If you've been working with agents locally and wondering what the next step looks like, this is it.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

speakerNames: Mahdi Ghodsi, Eda Zhou, Luke Berthot, Tyler Hofstede, Andrew Ruffin, Erica Zelaya
title: Build a custom AI agent with open-weight models and OpenClaw
sortTitle: build a custom ai agent with open-weight models and openclaw
description:
Build and run your own AI agent by hosting open-weight models on AMD GPU infrastructure. In this hands-on lab, you’ll assemble a tool-using agent, configure model hosting, and connect it to real workflows. Learn how to customize behavior, reduce API costs, and deploy agents with greater privacy and control using OpenClaw.

Join us for late night bytes!

Add this session to your schedule to reserve your seat, as capacity is limited. Please arrive at least 5 minutes early; unclaimed seats may be released to standby attendees.

title: Build an AI app with Azure SQL Hyperscale, Microsoft Fabric, & Foundry
sortTitle: build an ai app with azure sql hyperscale, microsoft fabric, & foundry
description:
Build an AI-powered FAQ assistant using Azure SQL Database vector search and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with Azure OpenAI Service. Use GitHub Copilot to speed T-SQL, mirror data into Microsoft Fabric (OneLake) for analytics, apply governance with Microsoft Purview, and orchestrate the workflow with Microsoft Foundry Agents.

speakerNames: Harmke Alkemade, Dimitri Maltezakis, Anton Slutsky, Hunter Bona, Zion Brewer, Gabriela Gonzalez-Do, Mary Howell
title: Build and deploy reasoning agents with NVIDIA Nemotron and Foundry
sortTitle: build and deploy reasoning agents with nvidia nemotron and foundry
description:
Build and deploy intelligent AI agents using NVIDIA AI-Q Blueprint and Nemotron with Microsoft Foundry, Azure Container Apps, and Azure AI Search. In this hands-on lab, you'll create an agent for enterprise deep research that combines AI reasoning, multimodal data extraction, and retrieval-augmented generation to synthesize data into actionable insights. Learn how to connect data sources and run inference at scale—then apply these patterns to your own production workflows.

This Lab is BYOD. Add this session to your schedule to reserve your seat, as capacity is limited. Please arrive at least 5 minutes early; unclaimed seats may be released to standby attendees.

title: Build with local AI agents using OpenCode and Lemonade
sortTitle: build with local ai agents using opencode and lemonade
description:
Harness the on-device power of the AMD Ryzen™ AI MAX+ processors  to run large coding models locally using Lemonade Server — no cloud, no API keys, all local. Learn how to architect specialized AI agents with OpenCode and watch them collaborate to build something you'll actually want to play. Buckle up - this one's going to be fun.

Note: This includes lunch before the Lab at Greens Restaurant—an iconic, seasonal, and organic dining experience—plan to arrive early and enjoy.

title: Build, deploy, and scale agents with Windows 365
sortTitle: build, deploy, and scale agents with windows 365
description:
Learn how Windows 365 delivers secure and preconfigured computing environments for AI agents, and how you can combine that with computer use to accelerate the creation and deployment of AI agents in enterprise environments. Through technical demos, this session shows how to set up Cloud PCs and how agentic AI interacts with live apps and UIs, while keeping governance, identity, and enterprise controls built in.

title: Create advanced Postgres-powered agentic apps with Azure HorizonDB
sortTitle: create advanced postgres-powered agentic apps with azure horizondb
description:
In this lab, you’ll build a production-ready AI agent using Azure HorizonDB as a unified data, retrieval, and intelligence layer. Implement RAG, hybrid semantic and keyword search, and agentic workflows without stitching external services. Use DiskANN advanced filtering and in-database AI model management to run LLMs and embeddings directly in the database - no vector stores or glue code. Ideal for developers and architects who want simpler stacks, higher performance, and faster AI delivery.

Add this session to your schedule to reserve your seat, as capacity is limited. Please arrive at least 5 minutes early; unclaimed seats may be released to standby attendees.

speakerNames: Clement Pakkam Isaac, Shuo-jen Wu, Alexander Zeltov, Hunter Bona, Zion Brewer, Gabriela Gonzalez-Do, Mary Howell
title: Create AI-powered video analytics apps with NVIDIA vision agents
sortTitle: create ai-powered video analytics apps with nvidia vision agents
description:
Build AI-powered video applications using the NVIDIA Video Search and Summarization (VSS) blueprint. In this hands-on lab, you’ll design vision agents that combine VLMs and LLMs to index, search, and summarize video streams, enabling natural language interaction with visual data at scale.

This Lab is BYOD. Add this session to your schedule to reserve your seat, as capacity is limited. Please arrive at least 5 minutes early; unclaimed seats may be released to standby attendees.
ogTitle: Create AI-powered video analytics apps with NVIDIA vision agents
ogDescription:

speakerNames: Julia Gvero, Julia Gvero, Rachel Kloster
title: From data to context: Agent‑ready knowledge with Foundry IQ
sortTitle: from data to context: agent‑ready knowledge with foundry iq
description:
What if your coding agent could reason over enterprise knowledge—across files, data, and work signals—without leaving your developer workflow? In this hands-on lab, you’ll build a reusable knowledge base in Foundry IQ using the Microsoft Foundry portal, expose it as an MCP server, and connect it to GitHub Copilot CLI—no custom RAG plumbing required. Experience how Foundry IQ and Copilot CLI work together to turn enterprise knowledge into live context, right where developers build and debug.

Add this session to your schedule to reserve your seat, as capacity is limited. Please arrive at least 5 minutes early; unclaimed seats may be released to standby attendees.

speakerNames: Yun Jung Choi, Lori Fraleigh, Julia Gvero, Rachel Kloster
title: From zero to deployed on Azure with AI agents
sortTitle: from zero to deployed on azure with ai agents
description:
What happens when you let AI agents do the building? In this hands-on lab, you'll go from an empty terminal to a deployed app on Azure — with GitHub Copilot CLI and coding agents handling the scaffolding, coding, debugging, and deployment. You'll use the new Azure skills to provision resources and wire up services through natural language, no portal required. This isn't a demo you watch. You'll walk out with a real, working dev workflow you can take straight to your next project.

Add this session to your schedule to reserve your seat, as capacity is limited. Please arrive at least 5 minutes early; unclaimed seats may be released to standby attendees.

speakerNames: Daniel Wang, Luke Berthot, Tyler Hofstede, Andrew Ruffin, Erica Zelaya
title: Generate video with PyTorch and ComfyUI: Build a GPU pipeline
sortTitle: generate video with pytorch and comfyui: build a gpu pipeline
description:
Build a video generation pipeline using open-source models and GPU acceleration. In this hands-on lab, you’ll use PyTorch and ComfyUI to generate video clips, configure workflows, and optimize performance. Learn how to run and customize video generation pipelines, reduce compute costs, and apply these techniques to real AI scenarios. Use your skills to participate in a challenge to win prizes.

Join us for the Bytes at Build reception after the Lab!

title: Get Started with Models in Microsoft Foundry to Build AI Apps
sortTitle: get started with models in microsoft foundry to build ai apps
description:
In this hands-on lab, you will build a production-ready AI application using Microsoft Foundry, with no fine-tuning or deep machine learning expertise required. You will discover and select models, provision a Foundry project, and connect to a hosted model using the OpenAI SDK. You’ll implement a comment moderation workflow, compare model outputs, and package the solution as a hosted agent using Python, ready for real-world integration.

Add this session to your schedule to reserve your seat, as capacity is limited. Please arrive at least 5 minutes early; unclaimed seats may be released to standby attendees.

title: Hands-on with the latest Cobalt VMs – from code to cloud deployment
sortTitle: hands-on with the latest cobalt vms – from code to cloud deployment
description:
Roll up your sleeves and get hands-on with the latest Azure Cobalt VMs. Provision your first Cobalt VM and explore what makes Arm tick in the Cloud. From there you'll build multi-arch container images with Docker, push them to ACR, and deploy to AKS clusters running on Cobalt VMs. Next, wire up a CI/CD pipeline that builds, tests, and deploys to Arm. Then go further—deploy a full-stack web app and serve AI predictions using ONNX Runtime on Arm. Walk out ready to build on Cobalt VMs.

Add this session to your schedule to reserve your seat, as capacity is limited. Please arrive at least 5 minutes early; unclaimed seats may be released to standby attendees.

From there you'll build multi-arch container images with Docker, push them to ACR, and deploy to AKS clusters running on Cobalt VMs. Next, wire up a CI/CD pipeline that builds, tests, and deploys to Arm. Then go further—deploy a full-stack web app and serve AI predictions using ONNX Runtime on Arm. Walk out ready to build on Cobalt VMs.

Add this session to your schedule to reserve your seat, as capacity is limited. Please arrive at least 5 minutes early; unclaimed seats may be released to standby attendees.

title: Improving agent behavior using reinforcement learning from traces
sortTitle: improving agent behavior using reinforcement learning from traces
description:
AI agents often work, but their behavior can be inconsistent or hard to control with prompts alone. In this hands-on lab, you will start with a working agent, inspect execution traces, define “good behavior” using evaluation graders, and apply reinforcement learning to reinforce better decisions. By the end, you’ll have a practical workflow for improving agent behavior using feedback from agent runs, without redesigning your agent from scratch.

Add this session to your schedule to reserve your seat, as capacity is limited. Please arrive at least 5 minutes early; unclaimed seats may be released to standby attendees.

speakerNames: Josh Johanning, Tiago Pascoal, Julia Gvero, Rachel Kloster
title: Make GitHub Copilot Work Your Way: Custom Tools, Context and Workflows
sortTitle: make github copilot work your way: custom tools, context and workflows
description:
GitHub Copilot is powerful out of the box, but custom agents let you shape it to fit your team's exact workflows. In this lab, you'll build agents from scratch: set their roles, write instructions, scope their tools, and plug them into real workflows. You'll create reusable Agent Skills for domain knowledge and connect agents to external services via MCP. Everything works across VS Code, Visual Studio, CLI, and Copilot coding agent. Leave with working agents you can adapt to your own codebase.

Add this session to your schedule to reserve your seat, as capacity is limited. Please arrive at least 5 minutes early; unclaimed seats may be released to standby attendees.

title: Observe, optimize and protect your hosted agents in Microsoft Foundry
sortTitle: observe, optimize and protect your hosted agents in microsoft foundry
description:
Modern agents fail in ways traditional monitoring can’t catch. In this hands-on lab, learn how Microsoft Foundry Observability helps you move from prototype → production with context-specific evaluation suites (auto-generated evaluators + test datasets) wired into developer workflows via skills/MCP tooling for hosted agents. Then scale quality with continuous evaluation, trace-linked analysis, and adaptive red teaming—and walk away with a sandbox to explore additional features on your own.

Add this session to your schedule to reserve your seat, as capacity is limited. Please arrive at least 5 minutes early; unclaimed seats may be released to standby attendees.

speakerNames: Daniel Han, Luke Berthot, Tyler Hofstede, Andrew Ruffin, Erica Zelaya
title: Reinforcement learning and local models on AMD Ryzen™ AI MAX+ with Unsloth
sortTitle: reinforcement learning and local models on amd ryzen™ ai max+ with unsloth
description:
Learn how to fine-tune AI models fully locally on AMD Ryzen™ AI MAX+ processors using Unsloth in this beginner to intermediate, instructor-led, hands-on workshop with no cloud required. Starting from dataset preparation and prompt formatting, you'll walk through the complete finetuning pipeline step by step. Along the way, you'll gain practical tips from industry experts and wrap up with a short challenge to put your freshly fine-tuned model to the test, all running on your own machine.

Note: This includes lunch after the Lab at Greens Restaurant—an iconic, seasonal, and organic dining experience.

Add this session to your schedule to reserve your seat, as capacity is limited. Please arrive at least 5 minutes early; unclaimed seats may be released to standby attendees.

title: Take LLMs from prototype to production on AKS
sortTitle: take llms from prototype to production on aks
description:
Moving an AI model from experiment to production is hard. Learn about AI Runway, an open-source accelerator that simplifies deploying LLMs on Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS). By treating models as native Kubernetes resources, AI Runway offers a single interface that adapts to multiple inference backends. You’ll deploy a production LLM on AKS, implement custom resources for scaling and networking, configure GPU and latency monitoring, and integrate it into CI/CD pipelines.

Add this session to your schedule to reserve your seat, as capacity is limited. Please arrive at least 5 minutes early; unclaimed seats may be released to standby attendees.

title: Terminal to Party: Live-Coding with GitHub Copilot CLI and Hydra
sortTitle: terminal to party: live-coding with github copilot cli and hydra
description:
Experience GitHub Copilot CLI while exploring OSS Hydra, a live-coding audio/visual environment that runs directly in the browser. Learn how to use agentic loops to create an AV party of your very own. EDM? Lo-fi? Have it all! You'll find your groove while exploring all that Copilot CLI has to offer, from planning, to managing multiple agents, to delegating longer tasks to the cloud. What better way to learn - and discover your new dev soundtrack! Warning. This program may contain flashing lights, strobe lights, loud noise, and/or smoke machines which may not be suitable for those with sensitivities.

Add this session to your schedule to reserve your seat, as capacity is limited. Please arrive at least 5 minutes early; unclaimed seats may be released to standby attendees.

title: Any agent, any cloud: Standardized tracing with Foundry+OpenTelemetry
sortTitle: any agent, any cloud: standardized tracing with foundry+opentelemetry
description:
Teams are shipping agents across clouds and frameworks—but telemetry is fragmented. In this demo, see how Microsoft Foundry and OpenTelemetry standards for GenAI tracing bring consistent observability that is agent framework agnostic and cloud agnostic. We’ll walk through the simple setup steps to instrument model and tool calls, quickly diagnose failures, latency, and cost spikes, and close the loop with trace-based evaluation, visualization and optimization.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: Beyond REST: .NET Apps That Talk to Hardware
sortTitle: beyond rest: .net apps that talk to hardware
description:
Most app demos stop at REST APIs. This live demo talks to real hardware. I’ll show a .NET app that discovers a device over BLE, exchanges data over NFC or serial, and turns raw device signals into a usable workflow. You’ll leave with a practical pattern for structuring device services, handling platform differences, and deciding when local connectivity beats the cloud.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: Build agentic apps in minutes with Rayfin and Microsoft Fabric
sortTitle: build agentic apps in minutes with rayfin and microsoft fabric
description:
Build an enterprise app in minutes with Rayfin, an agent-friendly, code-first backend-as-a-service. In this demo, we show how an AI agent generates a full stack web app with a managed database, authentication, and backend services. Learn how to connect the app to existing data in your Microsoft Fabric data estate, define services in code, and use Fabric’s built-in analytics, BI, and AI capabilities to power insights and intelligent experiences.

speakerNames: Jeff Vestal, Julia Gvero, Julia Gvero, Rachel Kloster, Lisa Troshian
title: Build context-aware agents using GitHub Copilot, Elastic, and Azure AI
sortTitle: build context-aware agents using github copilot, elastic, and azure ai
description:
Your AI reviewer has amnesia; it knows your code, but forgets the 2 AM outage from last month. We’ll demo giving Copilot a memory. Using Elastic Agent Builder and Workflows, we build an agent that reviews PRs against production telemetry and incident history. See it in action: an agent identifies a regression pattern, cites a past incident via OTel traces in Elasticsearch, and helps a dev fix it in VS Code via Copilot Agent Mode and the Elastic MCP server. Powered by Azure AI Foundry.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: Build work-ready agents with Foundry + Work IQ, govern with Agent 365
sortTitle: build work-ready agents with foundry + work iq, govern with agent 365
description:
An agent that knows nothing about your work is only half an agent. In this demo, we connect a Foundry agent to Work IQ via MCP, giving it real context about your work and ecosystem, governed by Agent 365. See it run under agent identity, watch Work IQ tools invoked at runtime, and get full observability on every call. Then we run side-by-side evals context-aware vs. context-blind, with scores that prove the difference. Leave knowing how to ground, govern, and evaluate your Foundry agents.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

speakerNames: Edo Segal, Tenille Bennett, Kristofer Ritzman, Ati Serpoushan, Gillian Sheldon
title: Create multimodal AI agents with persistent memory
sortTitle: create multimodal ai agents with persistent memory
description: Your users reach you through your website, your app, your store, your support line. And whether they were in touch five minutes or five days ago, every channel starts from zero, every time. Multimodal AI agents with persistent memory close that gap, and you can have them up and running now. In this session, Napster CTPO Edo Segal builds a working video AI agent live using the Napster Omniagent API. You'll leave with the architecture, the code patterns, and a clear next step to build your own agent that lives across every surface your organization touches.

title: Data Science & Machine Learning with Microsoft Fabric
sortTitle: data science & machine learning with microsoft fabric
description:
Explore Microsoft Fabric’s unified Data Science experience in this fast-paced 25-minute demo. See how to ingest and prepare data with OneLake and Lakehouse, build models using Notebooks, Spark, and SynapseML, and track experiments with MLflow. Learn how to operationalize models with batch scoring and integrate results into Power BI. The session also showcases building a simple generative AI Q&A solution using Fabric Data Agents, giving you a practical end-to-end view in a short time.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: Discovering Power App Data with M365 Copilot using MCP
sortTitle: discovering power app data with m365 copilot using mcp
description:
With millions of users world-wide, Power Apps and Dataverse now sit atop critical business data, but developers face challenges with discoverability and schema understanding. This session introduces Power Apps Model Context Protocol (MCP), which empowers M365 Copilot to access app metadata, Dataverse schemas, relationships, and permissions. Learn how MCP enables Copilot to answer natural-language queries, aggregate data, and deliver real-time insights, plus best practices for Copilot-ready apps.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

speakerNames: John Link, Viktor Veis, Julia Gvero, Julia Gvero, Rachel Kloster
title: Embracing frontier R&D with Microsoft Discovery
sortTitle: embracing frontier r&d with microsoft discovery
description: Microsoft Discovery is bringing Agentic Discovery to life.  Much as agentic software engineering is not just vibe coding, Agentic Discovery is not just equipping researchers with AI tools.  Agentic Discovery is a new operating model where autonomous teams of AI agents - guided by human expertise - execute entire cycles of the scientific method, from hypothesis formation to experimentation and reasoning.  Learn how Microsoft Discovery is making this vision reality today.

title: How Foundry integrates with open-source frameworks and tools
sortTitle: how foundry integrates with open-source frameworks and tools
description: This demo walks through building a practical OpenClaw like agent using open-source technologies and then operationalizing the same solution in Microsoft Foundry. You will see how to connect enterprise tools with the Model Context Protocol, codify repeatable behavior with skills, add browser automation with Playwright CLI, observe with OpenTelemetry, and move from local development to cloud-hosted agents using open protocols like Responses API and A2A to agent-to-agent communication.

title: OM1: Modular by Design, Hardware-Agnostic by Default. Your gateway to next-gen robotics
sortTitle: om1: modular by design, hardware-agnostic by default. your gateway to next-gen robotics
description: Robots today are powerful but siloed. Each one is locked to its own stack, hardware, and interfaces. OM1 is a modular, hardware-agnostic orchestration layer that bridges any robot with the cognitive infrastructure needed to make it actually useful. In this session, we'll walk through OM1's architecture, show how its modular design allows you to swap or add capabilities, and get started on your laptops. We'll also demo it live on the Unitree Go2 and in a cloud simulator. Bring a laptop if you'd like to follow along!

Passionate about sharing knowledge and making an impact in tech? Learn what it takes to become a Microsoft MVP from those who’ve done it and hear directly from MVP Program staff. Discover how to expand your influence, showcase your expertise, and practical tips for getting nominated. Walk away with a roadmap for growing your contributions and positioning yourself for MVP success.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: Supercharged profiling: Finding performance bugs with agents
sortTitle: supercharged profiling: finding performance bugs with agents
description:
Modern performance problems are harder than ever to diagnose: distributed systems, async code, and massive data make traditional profiling insufficient. In this demo-driven session, watch how Visual Studio combines advanced diagnostics with AI-powered agents to surface bottlenecks, explain root causes, and guide fixes faster than ever. From CPU and memory issues to “it only fails in production” bugs, you’ll see how profiling evolves in an agentic world.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: Teach AI agents to manage context with Elastic Agent Builder
sortTitle: teach ai agents to manage context with elastic agent builder
description:
Solve AI context limits by enabling agents to manage their own memory. Learn how to prevent bloated prompts and context drift during long tasks, reduce input token usage while ensuring enterprise data governance and scalability. Walk away with next steps for deploying the dynamically loaded skills, conversation context store, selective compaction, and secure external data connectors from Elasticsearch 9.4's Agent Builder  in the Microsoft ecosystem.

description:
What starts with a few people passionate about AI becomes a worldwide movement. Global AI Community grew from nothing into thousands of members, hundreds of events, and chapter leads on every continent, all volunteer-run, all free, all real.

title: Understand and fix Agent Framework apps with observability and evals
sortTitle: understand and fix agent framework apps with observability and evals
description:
Your AI apps are getting more complex, with multiple agents, tools, and different orchestration patterns. This makes them harder to understand, debug, and test. This session shows you how to visualize the decisions your LLMs are making in complex Microsoft Agent Framework applications, using open standards and open source tooling to provide you with instrumentation and observability. You'll also see how you can use another LLM as a judge to evaluate how well your AI app is working.

title: Agent Control Specification: Universal Control Layer for Safe Agents
sortTitle: agent control specification: universal control layer for safe agents
description: AI agents are rapidly gaining autonomy—but most still rely on prompts and ad hoc checks for safety. The Agent Control Specification introduces a universal runtime standard that adds enforceable guardrails across frameworks, models, and clouds. This talk shows how portable policy definitions can govern tool use, sensitive actions, approvals, and outputs in real time—turning agent safety from best effort into deterministic control without slowing innovation.

title: Agentic FinOps: Governing Cost and Quality with Microsoft Foundry
sortTitle: agentic finops: governing cost and quality with microsoft foundry
description:
Your agents are spending money you can't see. Traditional FinOps tooling was built for workloads humans start and stop, agents don't work that way. In this session, you'll learn how to use Microsoft Foundry's observability plane to get real-time visibility into token consumption, tool call chains, and reasoning traces, and how to turn that data into actual cost governance before your cloud bill becomes a surprise.

title: Agentic RAG for fast, low-cost knowledge retrieval on Azure Cosmos DB
sortTitle: agentic rag for fast, low-cost knowledge retrieval on azure cosmos db
description:
Skip the knowledge graph and ontology construction. The Agentic Retrieval Toolkit delivers high-quality answers to open-ended, multi-hop, and nuanced questions in seconds using GPU-accelerated local models or Microsoft Foundry models and Azure Cosmos DB — combining hybrid full-text and vector search, diversity selection, and sub-question decomposition across large structured and unstructured datasets. You'll leave knowing how to build production-ready knowledge retrieval as long-term agentic memory to boost your agents' accuracy and speed.

title: Agents Gone Wild: The Best (and Worst) AI Agents You Can Build Today
sortTitle: agents gone wild: the best (and worst) ai agents you can build today
description:
AI agents are everywhere—but not all of them should exist. In this session, we’ll explore how to build powerful, practical AI agents using Microsoft’s modern AI stack, including Azure AI services, Microsoft Foundry, and Copilot-ready integrations. Through real-world examples, we’ll look at useful agents such as a support assistant that summarizes customer tickets, a knowledge agent that answers questions from internal docs, and an automation agent that triages alerts from the cloud infrastructure.

title: Automate Windows on Arm migrations with AI-assisted specs
sortTitle: automate windows on arm migrations with ai-assisted specs
description:
Porting Windows apps to Arm used to take weeks: identifying dependencies, chasing compatibility gaps, and relying on trial and error. With AI, the same workflow now fits in a single session. This session shows Windows developers a spec-driven, AI-assisted workflow where a migration spec defines the architecture, GitHub Copilot applies Arm-aware changes, and GitHub Actions with WoA runners validate builds in CI. You’ll leave with a workflow that transforms your next migration from project to PR.

By the end of this lightning talk, you'll walk away with a number of practical ideas to take back to apply at work immediately.

sortTitle: broken, costly? debug and operate ai agents with azure monitor
description:
In this 15 minute session, we'll show how to debug and optimize your production agents. Start by instrumenting your agent with Application Insights instrumentation MCP. Then see it pay off in a new Agent Fleet view, where you can spot regressions across your subscription, agentic transaction maps, ROI and cost dashboards. Now you can move you from "what happened?" to "what do I do next?" Same OTel trace lights up Azure Monitor and Foundry for evals and human in the loop feedback.

sortTitle: build reliable coding agents with .net and durable workflows

title: Building your own MCP server
sortTitle: building your own mcp server
description:
The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is all the rage and rightly so. As the USB-C for AI, it is an incredibly powerful tool that acts as a bridge between real-world systems and LLMs. But how do you get started with MCP and more importantly: how do you build your own MCP server? In this session we'll build an MCP server from scratch using the MCP C# SDK. We'll look at how to build tools, prompts and resources and how to leverage these with our own AI agent in Microsoft Foundry.

title: Can LLMs generate high quality code?
sortTitle: can llms generate high quality code?
description:
AI tools are quickly becoming key contributors to the output of software engineering teams, but the code they write needs to be reliable, maintainable, and secure. Not all LLMs deliver that. Issues can also compound, and cause downstream problems. Our deep evaluation of LLMs surfaces their strengths and weaknesses. Learn how to pair AI agents with deterministic analysis to allow you to manage quality and security risks without giving up the productivity gains that made AI worth adopting.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: Fire, forget, sleep. VS Code agents that run without you
sortTitle: fire, forget, sleep. vs code agents that run without you
description:
You can only be in one place at once. Your agents don't have that problem. Copilot agents in VS Code run three ways — local for interactive pairing, background for isolated tasks in worktrees, and cloud for autonomous long-running jobs. See when to reach for each, how to hand off between them, and manage parallel sessions from a single view. Custom agents, subagent orchestration, and delegating a local plan to background or cloud execution are all on the table.

Legacy systems hold decades of critical business logic — but they’re opaque, hard to evolve, and nearly impossible to instrument properly. In this session, you’ll see how uses AI to do what used to take years: intelligently discover your application’s business domains and dependencies, extract the rules buried inside COBOL programs, and generate a modern, decoupled application ready for the cloud-native world.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: From Code to Agents: Build Production MCP Servers on Azure Functions
sortTitle: from code to agents: build production mcp servers on azure functions
description:
Agents are transforming how we build software but taking them from prototype to production remains the biggest challenge enterprise teams face. AI Agents need to call real tools- and today that means fragile, custom integrations that break when anything changes. MCP gives agents a standard way to discover and invoke tools at runtime. See real-world scenarios such as a DevOps CI/CD automation system where agents identify detect deployment issues, diagnose, and create rollback PRs in Azure DevOps.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

speakerNames: Ayca Bas, Julia Gvero, Rachel Kloster
title: From fragile demos to production agents with Foundry Toolkit
sortTitle: from fragile demos to production agents with foundry toolkit
description: Building AI agents is easy to demo, but hard to iterate, debug, and ship with confidence. Prompts, logic, and deployment often live in different tools, making it difficult to understand behavior or catch regressions. This talk shows how to design, build, and refine agents in VS Code with Foundry Toolkit and Copilot, evaluate and trace their behavior, and deploy to Microsoft Foundry—so prototypes can evolve into production without rework.

title: From kernel to agent: Build on-device AI with 2-bit models
sortTitle: from kernel to agent: build on-device ai with 2-bit models
description:
Running AI models locally is key to reducing latency, cost, and power usage—but model size remains a constraint. This session explores the engineering behind Microsoft and Qualcomm's 2-bit quantization. Learn how extreme quantization preserves model quality while dramatically reducing memory footprint. Understand how to use Windows ML APIs to load, run, and orchestrate quantized models locally on an AI PC to develop agentic AI applications.

title: Hidden gems of Microsoft tools: Dev Proxy and Dev Tunnel
sortTitle: hidden gems of microsoft tools: dev proxy and dev tunnel
description:
In this session, you’ll discover two lesser-known Microsoft tools: Dev Proxy and Dev Tunnel. Learn how Dev Proxy enables reality-driven development by simulating failures, mock responses and giving you control over real-world conditions. You’ll also see how Dev Tunnel lets you expose locally running services to the cloud, making it easy to prototype, test and collaborate across environments. Walk away with practical techniques to build resilient applications and unlock new development workflows.

speakerNames: Jeff Blankenburg, Sean Odell, Maddie Wambach
title: Make sure you understand the code AI is writing
sortTitle: make sure you understand the code ai is writing
description:
Copilot ships code fast. But neither you nor your agents understand what it does in production. That gap is growing. The old loop still looks like:
deploy → dashboards → logs → redeploy

It wasn’t built for AI-paced development. In this lightning talk, we break a real service in VS Code, then fix it using Copilot agent mode grounded in Dynatrace MCP. See runtime context flow into your editor so you and your agents can work in context and fix issues at the speed code is created.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: prompts.chat: Building an Open Prompt Ecosystem for the Age of Agents
sortTitle: prompts.chat: building an open prompt ecosystem for the age of agents
description:
Agents are getting more capable, but prompts still matter. The difference is prompts are no longer one-off inputs. They are becoming reusable building blocks for workflows, tools, and shared AI systems. I’ll share the story of prompts.chat and how it evolved from an open-source prompt library into a broader ecosystem, why prompts still matter in the age of agents, how they help define intent and context, and why open systems are important for collaboration, reuse, and better developer workflows.

title: Run AI coding agents on-device with NPUs
sortTitle: run ai coding agents on-device with npus
description:
AI-powered coding agents are transforming developer workflows, but most run in the cloud, adding latency, cost, and privacy concerns. What if your coding agent ran entirely on your laptop’s NPU? In this session, you'll learn how Visual Studio AI Toolkit and Qualcomm’s Hexagon NPU enable fully on device agentic coding. Using optimized SLMs, you'll experience real time code generation with no cloud calls, delivering high performance per watt, longer battery life, and responsive developer UX.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: Secure AI Agents in Azure: AI Gateway, Tools, and Trust

In this live demo, we’ll protect agent-to-tool and agent-to-API interactions using Azure API Management as an AI gateway, govern MCP tools and registries with API Center, enforce identity and secret hygiene with Entra ID and Key Vault, and add observability for tracing and audit. AI-driven insights help reduce food waste, improve safety, and enable smarter supply-chain decisions at scale.

title: Stop vulnerabilities in AI-generated code before they ship
sortTitle: stop vulnerabilities in ai-generated code before they ship
description:
Apps ship in hours with Copilot and Cursor. Vibes are great, until the vulns show up. AI code introduces new risks: hallucinated packages and insecure patterns. This session breaks down Aikido's real-world research on why traditional AppSec fails 'vibe coding' and how attackers exploit this shift. We’ll cover AI vuln patterns, execution vectors, supply chain risks, and practical guardrails. Vibe coding is inevitable; shipping vulns isn’t. Walk away with a security blueprint.

speakerNames: Roelant Dieben, Julia Gvero, Rachel Kloster
title: Stop Writing Prompts. Start Writing Specs.
sortTitle: stop writing prompts. start writing specs.
description:
Prompt engineering got us started, but it often leads to fragile systems that are hard to maintain and do not inspire confidence. In this session, we explore a different path. Using GitHub Spec Kit we build an agent from a specification, treating the spec as the contract that defines capabilities, tools, and outputs. Instead of rewriting prompts, we evolve the system by evolving the spec.

speakerNames: Kat Bose, Jess Brown, Syl Giuliani, Julia McClellan, Emma Webb
title: We thought AI transformation was about adopting agents. We were wrong.
sortTitle: we thought ai transformation was about adopting agents. we were wrong.
description:
We have data showing AI agents are improving at a super-exponential rate. If this continues, we'll likely have agents 10x more capable than the ones we had in November 2025 — within a year.

In this talk, we'll cover three myths about AI-Native transformations and how Augment transformed itself over the last three months. We've been blogging openly about this journey. You'll get the whole story and the learnings — so you can do the same for your organization.

sortTitle: why github copilot misses context (and how to fix it)

Rules, skills, prompt files, custom agents — see how they fit and when to reach for each.

title: Adding AI to Real Apps: The Agentic Way
sortTitle: adding ai to real apps: the agentic way
description:
Most teams aren’t building AI apps from scratch—they’re adding AI to applications that already exist. And increasingly, they’re not writing that integration themselves. This Table Talk explores how agentic development workflows—Copilot CLI, agent mode, and cloud coding agents—are reshaping how AI capabilities get added to real apps. We’ll discuss practical integration patterns, where agents accelerate the work, and where they still need a human in the loop. Bring your own modernization stories as we compare what’s working, what isn’t, and where to start.

title: AI Observability: What You Can’t See (and Why It Breaks)
sortTitle: ai observability: what you can’t see (and why it breaks)
description:
Traditional monitoring can’t catch LLM hallucinations or hidden token costs. In this discussion, we’ll move beyond "green" status lights to the architectural reality of running AI at scale. We will dive into Microsoft Foundry to explore correlating signals across infra, data, and model layers. Bring your production "war stories" as we debate how to use Azure AI Evaluation and tracing to build systems that are truly debuggable, measurable, and continuously improvable.

title: Any agent, any cloud: Observability patterns that scale
sortTitle: any agent, any cloud: observability patterns that scale
description:
Agents ship across frameworks and clouds—but telemetry is often fragmented. Let’s map an observability plan: what to trace (model + tool calls), what to measure (latency, cost, quality), and how to optimize (using trace-linked insights). Bring your scenario; we’ll compare a few viable patterns, debate tradeoffs, and leave with a practical checklist of next actions for your stack.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: Build & modernize multi-tenant AI app data planes for planetary scale
sortTitle: build & modernize multi-tenant ai app data planes for planetary scale
description:
Most AI-agent demos fail in production because their data platforms were never built for multi-tenant scale. This table talk connects two real journeys: modernizing relational systems to NoSQL, and running large-scale agentic SaaS. We’ll whiteboard migration tradeoffs, tenant-aware modeling, agent memory and retrieval patterns, plus hard lessons learned to ship reliable AI systems at scale today!

title: Building with AI on Systems You Don’t Fully Understand
sortTitle: building with ai on systems you don’t fully understand
description:
Most teams aren’t building on greenfield; they’re layering AI onto messy, poorly understood systems. What breaks when developers and AI agents lack visibility into system architecture and logic? In this discussion, we’ll compare approaches teams use today: reverse engineering, documentation methods, and queryable system intelligence. We’ll examine tradeoffs in speed, accuracy, and risk, and share ways to make systems more navigable for humans and AI so you can ship faster with fewer surprises.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: Claws on Windows: Designing Safe, Bounded Agent Actions
sortTitle: claws on windows: designing safe, bounded agent actions
description:
Claws touch files, processes, networks, and user state. Poorly designed claws create security risk and brittle behavior. In this interactive table talk, two experts will break down real claw designs used today, including permission scoping, execution boundaries, observability, and rollback. Developers will compare approaches, challenge assumptions, and share what has failed in practice while shaping clearer guidance for building reliable claws on Windows.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: Coding Agents in the Enterprise: What's Working? What's Not?
sortTitle: coding agents in the enterprise: what's working? what's not?
description:
With AI-assisted development on the rise, teams are rethinking how they build apps. They're using agents to write code, connect to data, provision services, and set access policies, all within existing compliance requirements. A common thread we're hearing: teams are struggling to unleash coding agents safely and maintainably. In this table talk, we'd love to hear where agents create the most value for your team and what keeps you up at night. We'll share learnings and best practices.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: Designing AI Data Platforms on Azure Blob Beyond Retrieval
sortTitle: designing ai data platforms on azure blob beyond retrieval
description:
You have data in Azure Blob Storage. You add embeddings, connect to search, and ship. It works until you need to change the data itself. Curation, feature engineering, training, search, and analytics quickly spread data across multiple systems. This discussion explores how teams design AI data platforms on Azure beyond retrieval, comparing multi-system setups with approaches that treat Blob as the unified foundation for raw data, embeddings, and features, and the tradeoffs that come with each.

title: Experimentation to Scale: Navigating Real-World AI Transformation
sortTitle: experimentation to scale: navigating real-world ai transformation
description:
AI is everywhere—but scaling it across organizations is still hard.

In this roundtable, we’ll move beyond hype to discuss the real challenges developers face when helping organizations adopt AI—from integrating into existing architectures and workflows to managing risk, governance, and change.

Join peers to share lessons learned, explore what’s working (and what isn’t), and uncover practical approaches to:
•	Moving from pilots to production
•	Enabling responsible and secure AI adoption
•	Driving alignment between engineering, business, and customers

This is an interactive session designed for developers actively shaping how AI is implemented—both inside their organizations and the customers they serve.

title: Fake It or Mask It: Choosing the Right Data for Agent Simulation Environments
sortTitle: fake it or mask it: choosing the right data for agent simulation environments
description:
Agents need realistic worlds to train in, but the data behind them is rarely safe to use raw. Two paths dominate: synthetic data that mirrors production, or de-identified real data that keeps its messiness without exposing real people. Each makes different tradeoffs on fidelity, edge cases, governance, and generalization. Let's compare notes on where each shine, where it breaks, and where teams blend the two.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: From Locked-In to Liquid: Modernizing .NET Before the November 2026 EOL
sortTitle: from locked-in to liquid: modernizing .net before the november 2026 eol
description:
.NET 8 and 9 both hit end of life on November 10, 2026. With hundreds of repos to upgrade, manual migration won't cut it—and pointing agents at the problem without structure burns tokens and produces inconsistent results. Let's talk about how agents, given the right deterministic tools, can move your .NET portfolio from rigid and locked-in to liquid—continuously modern, with swappable components and routine upgrades instead of fire drills. Bring your migration questions and stack horror stories.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

speakerNames: Nish Anil, Hazem El-Hammamy, Ryan Frazier
title: Modernizing at scale with agents: Patterns, pitfalls, and tradeoffs
sortTitle: modernizing at scale with agents: patterns, pitfalls, and tradeoffs
description:
Agents promise to compress years of app modernization into quarters, but speed isn’t the only goal. Enterprises still need to prioritize the right apps, define agent autonomy, and balance velocity with risk and cost. In this discussion, we’ll examine how teams are sequencing portfolios, designing agent workflows, and enforcing guardrails, along with lessons on where agents deliver real value and where they don’t yet.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: Multi-Agent Systems: When (and When Not) to Use Them
sortTitle: multi-agent systems: when (and when not) to use them
description:
Are multi-agent systems a breakthrough or architectural debt? While the hype is high, many teams over-engineer their AI stacks, leading to increased cost, latency, and complexity. This Table Talk focuses on when multi-agent architectures add real value and when simpler patterns are the better choice. We’ll explore trade-offs between RAG and multi-agent designs and discuss how to maintain control in production systems.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: Multimodal data: Architecting pipelines that don’t break at scale
sortTitle: multimodal data: architecting pipelines that don’t break at scale
description:
Multimodal AI unlocks 90% of the world's data: video, audio, PDFs, sensor streams, etc. But processing it breaks traditional batch pipelines and leaves GPUs idle 50%+ of the time.

In this session, you'll learn:
-Why multimodal pipelines break traditional batch architectures
-How to eliminate CPU to GPU I/O bottlenecks
-How Anyscale built a streaming CPU+GPU engine with Ray Data
-How AV customers scale data-intensive workloads on Azure

For CV researchers, ML engineers, and physical AI teams.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: Outcome-maxxing, not token-maxxing: what "good" looks like in AI-authored code
sortTitle: outcome-maxxing, not token-maxxing: what "good" looks like in ai-authored code
description:
AI is now the primary author in most codebases. Throughput is climbing. Bugs, incidents, churn, and review time are climbing faster. Developers are optimizing for tokens accepted, not code that survives. A working session, not a panel. We'll compare how teams redefine "good" in AI-authored code: where to set the bar at authorship vs. review, which guardrails earn their friction, and what to track when dashboards say faster but the team feels slower. Bring an argument you're tired of having.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: Production MCP Servers: Auth, Tools, and the Patterns That Hold Up
sortTitle: production mcp servers: auth, tools, and the patterns that hold up
description:
Custom agents and platforms like Copilot are only as useful as the actions they can take in the systems that matter. That requires production-grade MCP: secure auth, reliable tools, and the governance the spec leaves to you. We'll cover what holds up under real workloads and the patterns that don't. Bring the problems you're stuck on.

title: Rethinking Retrieval with Foundry IQ
sortTitle: rethinking retrieval with foundry iq
description:
Most teams build retrieval the same way: custom pipelines on top of search. Foundry IQ aims to improve and changes that. This discussion explores traditional vs agentic retrieval, showing where accuracy improves and where latency tradeoffs appear. We’ll walk through real use cases, built-in multi-retrieval capabilities, and how Foundry IQ integrates with Azure AI Search and other IQ layers. The focus is on when to use it, what it replaces, and how to adopt it without rebuilding everything.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: Scaling PostgreSQL for Agentic Apps: Patterns and Tradeoffs
sortTitle: scaling postgresql for agentic apps: patterns and tradeoffs
description:
Agentic apps place new demands on PostgreSQL - low-latency retrieval, the need to combine graph, transactional, and document models within a single system, integration with AI models. In this Table Talk, explore architecture patterns for agentic development with PostgreSQL using Azure Horizon DB. Compare approaches for embedding pipelines, agentic architectures - balancing performance, cost, and scale - and learn where designs break in production.

title: Shifting accessibility left in AI code
sortTitle: shifting accessibility left in ai code
description:
Explore what it means to shift accessibility left in your engineering pipeline so innovation scales inclusion by default. We’ll open the discussion to share perspectives on what’s working, where gaps remain, and how you and your teams can embed accessibility into AI tooling and engineering practices.

title: Shipping at the Speed of Ideas: Real Lessons in AI-First Development
sortTitle: shipping at the speed of ideas: real lessons in ai-first development
description:
When the full stack goes from concept to live in hours, software development changes in ways not obvious until you're in it. We learned this firsthand building an AI software creation platform, and see it play out daily. This session shares hard lessons from both sides: engineering a system that writes reliable code, and patterns that succeed and fail when developers go AI-first. Walk away knowing where to trust the agent, where to stay in control, and how to build things to last.

title: The Legacy Java Problem No One Wants to Inherit
sortTitle: the legacy java problem no one wants to inherit
description:
Every team has one: the Java app no one fully understands, running a runtime, no one wants to touch, with tests that may or may not exist. Modernizing it used to mean months of planning and a careful rewrite. Now AI agents are quietly changing the math. This Table Talk explores how teams are tackling legacy Java modernization with agentic tools—analyzing unfamiliar codebases, planning upgrades, and refactoring at a pace that wasn’t possible a year ago. We’ll trade stories on what’s working, what’s breaking, and how the role of the developer is shifting in the process.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: Trusted AI Built for Production
sortTitle: trusted ai built for production
description:
Responsible AI is widely discussed, but hard to operationalize. Many teams struggle to move AI systems from MVP to production. This session covers defining risk thresholds (safety, jailbreaks, accuracy, hallucinations), generating test cases with LLMs, and building golden datasets. We’ll discuss evaluating multi-turn agentic systems using Azure evaluation SDKs and AI Red Teaming Agent, plus integration into CI/CD for repeatable, production-ready systems across prompts, models, and agents.

title: What AI Can’t Replace: The Enduring Value of Human Judgment, Trust, and Connection
sortTitle: what ai can’t replace: the enduring value of human judgment, trust, and connection
description:
As AI agents take on more planning, execution, and coordination, human contribution is shifting—not disappearing. This session explores what remains uniquely human in an agentic world, and why those capabilities will increase in value.

We’ll examine where human judgment outperforms machine optimization, how trust is built in AI-mediated systems, and why connection and accountability can’t be fully virtualized.

Join the conversation to rethink the future of work, product design, and leadership—focusing on where human value matters most.

Seating for this session is first-come, first-served. Add it to your schedule to plan your day and arrive early to secure a spot.

title: Why is debugging distributed, complex production systems still this hard?
sortTitle: why is debugging distributed, complex production systems still this hard?
description:
Azure native applications built on AKS produce logs, metrics, and traces across highly distributed systems. During an incident, correlating that data across services, dependencies, and recent changes becomes the hardest part of the problem. This discussion focuses on how developers approach that challenge today and how it can be improved.

title: Agentic infrastructure needs agentic observability
sortTitle: agentic infrastructure needs agentic observability
description: Observability pipelines were built for a world where engineers manually standardized logs, traces, and metrics. That model is breaking down as AI generates services and infrastructure faster than pipelines can keep up. Explore a new model where observability layers reason over telemetry and adapt as systems evolve. Agent-driven workflows continuously interpret and normalize data, raising new questions around guardrails, validation, and autonomy.
aiDescription:
Introduction – The End of Human-Centric Observability: At the beginning of the talk 00:00:01–00:00:49, Jimmy Herbert introduces a fundamental shift in the world of software operations. He recalls how observability tools were historically designed for human operators to debug and understand systems through logs, traces, dashboards, and alerts. That model, he argues, is breaking down because the consumers of observability are no longer people but intelligent agents capable of writing and executing code autonomously. As he declares, “agentic infrastructure needs agentic observability,” marking the thematic foundation for the rest of his presentation. He clarifies that this is not a product pitch but a systemic diagnosis of what’s failing in traditional observability since AI entered the software lifecycle 00:00:49–00:01:20.

Problem One – Logs and Understanding Agent Reasoning: Herbert begins diagnosing the first major issue—logging. Traditional logs were designed for discrete, human-readable events like "request completed" or "database failed" 00:01:30–00:01:50. But intelligent agents generate something different: decision breadcrumbs, or as he terms it, "reasoning trails." These trails involve document retrievals, relevance scoring, retries, and contextual reevaluation, all across long time horizons 00:01:55–00:02:31. The challenge shifts from asking “what happened?” to “why did the agent decide this?” Current observability platforms aren’t designed to answer such questions because they assume deterministic systems with reconstructable narratives. In reality, similar inputs now yield different outcomes, and understanding the reasoning path has become more critical than tracking technical execution 00:02:45–00:03:10.

Problem Two – Traces, Sampling, and the End of 200 OK: The second major issue Herbert identifies concerns traces and sampling strategies. He explains that traditional application performance monitoring (APM) assumes a deterministic world; however, “sampling breaks and status codes lie” 00:03:14. A random trace from an agentic workflow provides little insight because crucial reasoning steps may be excluded. Tail-based sampling also fails, since retaining thousands of spans in memory is computationally unscalable 00:03:37–00:03:54. Herbert provocatively points out that a “200 OK means nothing” in this new world; it only confirms that nothing crashed, not that the outcome was correct 00:04:00–00:04:20. He describes how agents can hallucinate or misinterpret data despite producing successful infrastructure responses. Moreover, the explosion of telemetry from agentic systems threatens cost models and forces undesirable trade-offs between complete visibility and affordability 00:04:44–00:05:18.

Problem Three – Sensitivity, Instrumentation, and Human Limits: Moving into the third challenge 00:05:27–00:06:11, Herbert dives into the growing sensitivity of telemetry. Modern AI workloads embed prompts, personal data, and proprietary business logic within traces and logs. What once were technical artifacts now contain deeply private information, transforming telemetry into a liability rather than a cost item. Simultaneously, the practice of manual instrumentation collapses under the pace of AI-generated artifacts 00:06:08–00:06:22. He argues for observability that instruments itself automatically and can keep pace with systems evolving faster than humans. This leads to a stark realization: traditional observability engineering has been optimized around human cognitive limits—reducing data complexity, simplifying dashboards, and filtering noise—but these optimizations now constrain AI agents that thrive on more data, more context, and more completeness 00:06:42–00:07:11.

Agentic Architecture – Rebuilding Observability: Herbert then discusses how observability must evolve into an architecture designed for data abundance rather than scarcity 00:07:16–00:08:01. He introduces Groundcover’s approach, emphasizing three principles: BYOC (Bring Your Own Cloud) to keep sensitive data under customer control, zero instrumentation for low operational friction, and unified visibility for both human- and AI-written code 00:08:47–00:09:46. Central to this architecture is a separation between the model and the analytic engine. The LLM should handle understanding intent and orchestration, while deterministic back-end analytics handle computation 00:09:50–00:10:30. This division allows investigation to move from manual navigation to AI-driven goal-based diagnosis—agents can autonomously find the problem, explain what went wrong, and even initiate corrective actions 00:10:47–00:11:40.

Conclusion – Observability as the Agentic Operating System: In his closing remarks 00:12:06–00:14:00, Herbert raises governance concerns: how much autonomy should these observability agents have, who approves their interventions, and who holds accountability for automated fixes gone wrong? Despite the uncertainties, he predicts that companies mastering “agentic observability” will hold a structural advantage. The overarching principle—“AI cannot fix what it cannot see”—encapsulates his thesis. Observability, he concludes, is transforming into the operating system for the agentic software development lifecycle (SDLC). It will become not merely a passive monitoring layer but an active, intelligent participant in understanding and maintaining modern software systems—a necessary foundation for any AI-driven infrastructure to function effectively.
ogTitle: Agentic infrastructure needs agentic observability
ogDescription: Observability pipelines were built for a world where engineers manually standardized logs, traces, and metrics. That model is breaking down as AI generates services and infrastructure faster than pipelines can keep up. Explore a new model where observability layers reason over telemetry and adapt as systems evolve. Agent-driven workflows continuously interpret and normalize data, raising new questions around guardrails, validation, and autonomy.

title: AI Building Blocks for .NET: Add intelligence to your C# apps
sortTitle: ai building blocks for .net: add intelligence to your c# apps
description: A practical, opinionated guide to building intelligent apps in .NET.
aiDescription:
Introduction and Overview: The video opens with Bruno welcoming viewers and introducing the session on AI in .NET 00:00:00. He explains that the goal is to explore AI building blocks provided by Microsoft for developers and demonstrates their integration using a showcase application, a full support center app that analyzes incidents and user feedback 00:00:31. Bruno mentions that the application runs with Aspire coordination, showing how different services interact including a Blazor web UI, a Microsoft Agent Framework agent for image generation, and an NVIDIA Nemo agent for data analysis 00:02:11. He introduces Aspire traces to monitor service interactions and sets the context for the upcoming deep dive into the .NET AI architecture.

Microsoft Extensions for AI and Local Deployment: Bruno then introduces Microsoft Extensions for AI, explaining its role as a chat abstraction layer that allows easy interaction with large language models (LLMs) via standardized interfaces 00:03:41. He showcases sample applications using Azure Foundry with integrated security, avoiding the need for API keys while maintaining authentication with CLI credentials 00:06:22. A live demo illustrates switching between models—like GPT-5 and Kimi—and retrieving model-specific responses. Bruno moves on to demonstrate running local models using Ollama 00:08:10, performing sentiment analysis locally through the same interface. He emphasizes how Microsoft’s chat client can connect seamlessly to either cloud or local models, maintaining consistent workflows across environments 00:10:09.

Data and Embeddings Fundamentals: Moving deeper, Bruno explains using data within AI applications, particularly Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), which involves reading, chunking, embedding, and storing text 00:11:01. He demonstrates how embeddings convert text into vector representations for semantic search and shows an example where movie descriptions are embedded in memory for querying 00:14:09. By asking questions like “find a family-friendly movie with dragons,” the app accurately retrieves related content such as “Shrek” or “The Matrix.” He also presents a Markdown data ingestion pipeline 00:20:04, detailing how documents can be read, chunked, summarized, and written into a vector store using SQLite. This section demonstrates the scalable flexibility of working with embeddings locally or in the cloud, bridging unstructured data to semantic search capabilities through .NET AI libraries.

Integrating External Tools with MCP: Bruno transitions to introducing the Model Context Protocol (MCP), a standard enabling AI applications to access external tools and APIs 00:23:01. He demonstrates a console app connecting to the Microsoft Learn MCP server, showing how chat clients invoke MCP tools to retrieve live documentation and current framework versions from learn.microsoft.com 00:25:02. A side-by-side test shows how answers improve when connected via MCP, bringing live URLs and precise version details without manual linking 00:29:04. This integration showcases how MCP empowers AI clients to extend their capabilities beyond static responses, connecting dynamic data sources directly through .NET chat and embedding frameworks.

Agent Framework and Multi-Agent Workflows: The next segment focuses on Microsoft Agent Framework, extending Microsoft Extensions for AI 00:33:09. Bruno explains how agents are created atop chat clients by adding descriptive instructions, transforming them into autonomous entities capable of writing, editing, or generating content 00:35:47. Through examples, he shows how agents collaborate by sharing outputs sequentially in workflows, powered by built-in interfaces like IAgent and visualized using the Dev UI tool. He introduces Agent-to-Agent (A2A) protocol support for cross-agent communication, illustrating NVIDIA Nemo and Microsoft agents interacting via coordinated URLs 00:39:54. The ability to link multiple models, external APIs, and internal logic via agents and workflows demonstrates the full modular power of .NET AI integrations.

Conclusion and Resources: In closing, Bruno summarizes the AI ecosystem in .NET encompassing chat interactions, embeddings, RAG pipelines, MCP integrations, and Agent Framework orchestration 00:42:39. He revisits the showcase application where all components—data analysis, image generation, and multi-agent collaboration—work together under Aspire coordination. Developers are encouraged to explore Microsoft's official libraries and documentation at aiappsfor.net and Generative AI for Beginners in .NET for extended learning. Bruno wraps up by thanking viewers, reinforcing that these libraries empower developers to build complex AI solutions natively in C#, with direct support for both cloud and local environments 00:43:45.
ogTitle: AI Building Blocks for .NET: Add intelligence to your C# apps
ogDescription: A practical, opinionated guide to building intelligent apps in .NET.
